mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a healthy and robust labor market, which supports strong consumer spending and corporate profitability. This positive economic backdrop is expected to drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains below 4.5% for a sustained period, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently negative (e.g., average return < -0.5% over any 12-month rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is significantly elevated, it signals a restrictive monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation. This increases borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, dampening economic activity and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS consistently remains above 5.0%, but S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Robust employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with strong retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a powerful and expanding consumer demand environment. This directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3mo avg growth > 0.1% AND RSAFS 3mo avg growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: If both PAYEMS and RSAFS consistently show strong growth as per the trigger, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, a significant rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) signals increasing manufacturing activity, business investment, and capacity utilization. This improvement in the supply side of the economy is highly supportive of corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows strong recovery during a confirmed post-recession period, but S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently flat or negative (average return < 0.0%) over the subsequent 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals a contraction in the construction sector and broader economic demand. This leads to reduced investment, lower consumer confidence, and ultimately negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST declines significantly as per the trigger, but S&P 500 20-day returns remain positive or flat (average return > 0.0%) in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power for consumers and compresses corporate profit margins. This often prompts tighter monetary policy, collectively exerting downward pressure on S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL consistently remains above 4.0% year-over-year, but S&P 500 consistently delivers strong positive 60-day returns (average return > 1.5%) over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapidly rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) coupled with declining non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a severe deterioration in the labor market. This signals an imminent or ongoing recession, significantly depressing corporate earnings and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS 3mo change < -0.2%
failure_condition: If both UNRATE and PAYEMS show significant deterioration as per the trigger, but S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects a healthy and expanding economy, indicating strong aggregate demand and business activity. This provides a robust fundamental backdrop for corporate profitability and thus supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 shows consistent quarter-over-quarter growth above 2.0% (annualized), but S&P 500 consistently delivers negative 5-day returns (average return < -0.2%) over any 12-month rolling window.
